item.title: Delta blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: Cultural capital
item.description: Two children walk into a museum on a school trip.

item.title: Cultural omnivorousness
item.description: A philosophy professor who keeps a Dolly Parton record next to the Bach on the shelf would have puzzled the sociologist of 1970.

item.title: Bourdieu — Distinction
item.description: Author: Pierre Bourdieu Title: Distinction: A Social Critique of the Judgement of Taste Year: 1984 (French original, La Distinction, 1979) Publisher: Harvard University Press Type: Book Bourdieu’s 1960s French survey asked people which of three pieces of music they most admired — Bach’s The Well-Tem...

item.title: Lott — Love and Theft
item.description: Author: Eric Lott Title: Love and Theft: Blackface Minstrelsy and the American Working Class Year: 1993 Type: Academic book (Oxford University Press) A study of blackface minstrelsy that reframes white America’s relationship with Black culture as simultaneously one of desire and domination.

item.title: Authenticity and its discontents
item.description: Simon Frith’s Performing Rites makes a claim that sounds simple and proves difficult to absorb: when a listener calls a record “authentic,” they are not detecting a quality the music possesses but applying a set of learned conventions about what authentic music should sound like.

item.title: Toynbee — Making Popular Music
item.description: Author: Jason Toynbee Title: Making Popular Music: Musicians, Creativity and Institutions Year: 2000 Publisher: Arnold (London) / Oxford University Press (New York) Type: Book Toynbee argues that the dominant model of musical creativity — the Romantic ideal of the solitary genius producing original ...

item.title: Dylan at Newport
item.description: Date: July 25, 1965 Location: Freebody Park, Newport, Rhode Island (Newport Folk Festival) Key figures: Bob Dylan, Mike Bloomfield, Al Kooper, Pete Seeger, Alan Lomax, Peter Yarrow, Albert Grossman On a Sunday evening in Newport, Bob Dylan walked onstage with a Fender Stratocaster and a five-piece e...
